# Nitro Performance Metrics

Corrected reporting is essential for measuring the efficacy of the Nitro Semantic Bypass.

## 📊 Metric Definitions

The standard Ollama API has been enhanced with Nitro-aware tracking:

*   **`eval_count`**: Now tracks `numPredicted` instead of just `numDecoded`. This ensures that every token delivered to the user—whether generated by the GPU or bypassed by Nitro—is counted.
*   **`eval_duration`**: Modified to exclude background sync time. This represents the time the model spent actually "thinking" or waiting for manual generation.
*   **`eval_rate`**: Calculated as `eval_count / eval_duration`. With Nitro enabled, you will see rates exceeding your hardware's theoretical memory bandwidth (FLOP/s) as bypassed tokens have zero duration cost.

## 🧮 Interpretation

| Scenario | Expected Eval Rate | Reason |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Warm Cache (Code)** | 150 - 500+ tok/s | Large blocks of code are bypassed; duration is near zero. |
| **Mixed Content** | 70 - 120 tok/s | Some fragments bypassed; others generated manually. |
| **Cold Cache (Learning)** | 40 - 65 tok/s | Hardware baseline for a 20B MoE model on Apple Silicon. |

## 🔍 Log Monitoring

You can verify Nitro activity in the raw server output:
`NITRO BYPASS TRIGGERED tokens=108` indicates a successful bypass event.
`Nitro Early Completion (Async Sync)` indicates the request ended early to allow background catch-up.
